title: Ensure 'Access Approval' is 'Enabled'

input_row.title: Ensure 'Access Approval' is 'Enabled'
input_row.description: GCP Access Approval enables you to require your organizations' explicit approval whenever Google support try to access your projects. You can then select users within your organization who can approve these requests through giving them a security role in IAM. All access requests display which Google Employee requested them in an email or Pub/Sub message that you can choose to Approve. This adds an additional control and logging of who in your organization approved/denied these requests.
input_row.rationale: Controlling access to your information is one of the foundations of information security. Google Employees do have access to your organizations' projects for support reasons. With Access Approval, organizations can then be certain that their information is accessed by only approved Google Personnel.
input_row.audit: From Google Cloud Console Determine if Access Transparency is Enabled as it is a Dependency 1. From the Google Cloud Home inside the project you wish to audit, click on the Navigation hamburger menu in the top left. Hover over the IAM & Admin Menu. Select settings in the middle of the column that opens. 2. The status should be "Enabled' under the heading Access Transparency Determine if Access Approval is Enabled 1. From the Google Cloud Home, within the project you wish to check, click on the Navigation hamburger menu in the top left. Hover over the Security Menu. Select Access Approval in the middle of the column that opens. 2. The status will be displayed here. If you see a screen saying you need to enroll in Access Approval, it is not enabled. From Google Cloud CLI Determine if Access Approval is Enabled 1. From within the project you wish to audit, run the following command. gcloud access-approval settings get 2. The status will be displayed in the output. IF Access Approval is not enabled you should get this output: API [accessapproval.googleapis.com] not enabled on project [-----]. Would you like to enable and retry (this will take a few minutes)? (y/N)? After entering Y if you get the following output, it means that Access Transparency is not enabled: ERROR: (gcloud.access-approval.settings.get) FAILED_PRECONDITION: Precondition check failed.
input_row.remediation: From Google Cloud Console 1. From the Google Cloud Home, within the project you wish to enable, click on the Navigation hamburger menu in the top left. Hover over the Security Menu. Select Access Approval in the middle of the column that opens. 2. The status will be displayed here. On this screen, there is an option to click Enroll. If it is greyed out and you see an error bar at the top of the screen that says Access Transparency is not enabled please view the corresponding reference within this section to enable it. 3. In the second screen click Enroll. Grant an IAM Group or User the role with permissions to Add Users to be Access Approval message Recipients 1. From the Google Cloud Home, within the project you wish to enable, click on the Navigation hamburger menu in the top left. Hover over the IAM and Admin. Select IAM in the middle of the column that opens. 2. Click the blue button the says + ADD at the top of the screen. 3. In the principals field, select a user or group by typing in their associated email address. 4. Click on the role field to expand it. In the filter field enter Access Approval Approver and select it. 5. Click save. Add a Group or User as an Approver for Access Approval Requests 1. As a user with the Access Approval Approver permission, within the project where you wish to add an email address to which request will be sent, click on the Navigation hamburger menu in the top left. Hover over the Security Menu. Select Access Approval in the middle of the column that opens. 2. Click Manage Settings 3. Under Set up approval notifications, enter the email address associated with a Google Cloud User or Group you wish to send Access Approval requests to. All future access approvals will be sent as emails to this address. From Google Cloud CLI 1. To update all services in an entire project, run the following command from an account that has permissions as an 'Approver for Access Approval Requests' gcloud access-approval settings update --project= -- enrolled_services=all --notification_emails='@'
input_row.impact: To use Access Approval your organization will need have enabled Access Transparency and have at one of the following support level: Enhanced or Premium. There will be subscription costs associated with these support levels, as well as increased storage costs for storing the logs. You will also not be able to turn the Access Transparency which Access Approval depends on, off yourself. To do so you will need to submit a service request to Google Cloud Support. There will also be additional overhead in managing user permissions. There may also be a potential delay in support times as Google Personnel will have to wait for their access to be approved.
input_row.default_value: By default Access Approval and its dependency of Access Transparency are not enabled.
input_row.additional_information: The recipients of Access Requests will also need to be logged into a Google Cloud account associated with an email address in this list. To approve requests they can click approve within the email. Or they can view requests at the the Access Approval page within the Security submenu.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_access_approval_enabled_check

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **GCP API calls or Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) SDK methods to use:**
  - Use `gcloud access-approval settings get` to retrieve the current settings of Access Approval.
  
- **GCP CLI commands (if applicable):**
  - The command `gcloud access-approval settings get --project=YOUR_PROJECT_ID` will return the current status of Access Approval for the specified project.
  
- **Specific resources to enumerate:**
  - This applies to the specific GCP projects where Access Approval needs to be checked.
  
- **Exact configuration properties/settings to validate:**
  - Verify that the output of the command shows that Access Approval is enabled. The expected output should indicate enrollment and listing of notification emails designated for Access Approval requests.
  
- **Clear Pass/Fail criteria with expected values:**
  - If Access Approval is enabled, the output will not show any error regarding the API being disabled and will detail the enrolled services and notification emails. 
  - If Access Approval is not enabled, it should produce an error indicating the API is not enabled on the project or that enrollment details are not properly set.

- **Example:** 
  - Use Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) with the command `gcloud access-approval settings get` to check if Access Approval is enabled for the specific GCP project. Valid results would confirm that the project is enrolled with the corresponding settings, while an error output would indicate a lack of activation.
step2_review:
**APPROACH VALIDATION:** AGREE
- The classification of the audit approach as "Automated" is correct. Access Approval settings can be checked using the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) or GCP CLI, making automation feasible. The use of `gcloud access-approval settings get` is appropriate for retrieving the current settings.

**TECHNICAL ACCURACY:** 8
- The automation details are mostly accurate. The command `gcloud access-approval settings get --project=YOUR_PROJECT_ID` is valid for checking Access Approval settings. However, the assessment could benefit from specifying the expected output format more clearly, such as detailing specific fields that indicate Access Approval is enabled.
- The program name `gcp_access_approval_enabled_check` is somewhat descriptive but could be improved for clarity and specificity.

**IMPROVEMENTS:**
- The assessment could include a more detailed explanation of the expected output fields, such as `enrolledServices` and `notificationEmails`, to confirm that Access Approval is enabled.
- Consider including a check for organization-level settings using `gcloud access-approval settings get --organization=YOUR_ORG_ID` to ensure comprehensive coverage.
- The program name could be improved to `gcp_access_approval_enrollment_check` to more accurately reflect that it checks for enrollment status and settings.
- It might be beneficial to mention any IAM roles required to execute the command successfully, ensuring the user has the necessary permissions.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH
- I am confident in the assessment's approach and technical details, with minor improvements suggested for clarity and completeness.

**FINAL RECOMMENDATION:**
- Modify: Enhance the assessment by specifying expected output fields for clarity, consider organization-level checks, and improve the program name for better specificity.

step3_final_decision.decision_text:
**FINAL APPROACH:** Automated

**JUSTIFICATION:** Both AI opinions agree that the audit for ensuring 'Access Approval' is enabled can be automated using the Google Cloud SDK (gcloud). The command `gcloud access-approval settings get --project=YOUR_PROJECT_ID` effectively retrieves the current settings of Access Approval, making it possible to automate the compliance check. The necessary properties are exposed through GCP APIs, allowing for a straightforward automated approach.

**CONFIDENCE:** HIGH

---

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_access_approval_enrollment_check

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **GCP Resource/Service:** Access Approval
- **API/CLI Method:** Google Cloud SDK (gcloud) `access-approval settings get`
- **Properties to Check:** `enrolledServices`, `notificationEmails`
- **Pass Criteria:** The output should confirm that the project is enrolled with Access Approval, showing the `enrolledServices` and listing `notificationEmails`.
- **Fail Criteria:** The output indicates that Access Approval is not enabled, such as missing `enrolledServices` or errors stating the API is not enabled.

**DISAGREEMENTS RESOLVED:** There were no significant disagreements between the AI opinions. Both agreed on the feasibility of automation using the GCP CLI, and the second opinion provided additional suggestions for improving the program name and detailing expected output fields, which have been incorporated into the final decision.

**SOURCE:** CIS_Google_Cloud_Platform_Foundation_Benchmark_v4_0_0
